{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Service Center Warehouse Officer",
  "description": "OPPO, founded in 2004, is one of the world&rsquo;s leading innovators in smart devices and mobile technology. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, Dedicated to delivering cutting-edge innovation, premium user experiences, and stylish designs. OPPO continues to expand its footprint with over 290,000 points of sale and 1,900 official service centers &mdash; bringing the beauty of technology closer to people every day.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Service Center Warehouse Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Inventory Management: Oversee daily stock levels, conduct physical and cycle counts, update inventory records (bin cards, ledgers), and resolve discrepancies.
	Receiving &amp; Dispatch: Supervise incoming deliveries, verify quality and quantity against orders, prepare goods for storage and shipment, and ensure accurate dispatch.
	Operations &amp; Coordination: Maintain proper warehouse layout, ensure equipment is functional, liaise with sales, logistics, and suppliers, and provide regular status reports.
	Quality &amp; Safety: Inspect goods for damage, ensure compliance with safety standards, and maintain a clean and organized warehouse.
	Staff Supervision (if applicable): Support the Warehouse Manager in supervising warehouse staff and enforcing standard operating procedures.


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of a High School Diploma with relevant experience in stock or warehouse management.
	Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in computer usage, MS Office (Excel, Word), and inventory management software.
	Ability to multitask, work under pressure, and operate in a physically demanding environment.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.


Salary
N50,000 - N70,000 / month

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;cvpool2021@oppo-aed.ng&nbsp;using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.",

Service Center Warehouse Officer Job at OPPO




        
    

        
        OPPO, founded in 2004, is one of the world’s leading innovators in smart devices and mobile technology. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, Dedicated to delivering cutting-edge innovation, premium user experiences, and stylish designs. OPPO continues to expand its footprint with over 290,000 points of sale and 1,900 official service centers — bringing the beauty of technology closer to people every day.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Service Center Warehouse Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Inventory Management: Oversee daily stock levels, conduct physical and cycle counts, update inventory records (bin cards, ledgers), and resolve discrepancies.
	Receiving & Dispatch: Supervise incoming deliveries, verify quality and quantity against orders, prepare goods for storage and shipment, and ensure accurate dispatch.
	Operations & Coordination: Maintain proper warehouse layout, ensure equipment is functional, liaise with sales, logistics, and suppliers, and provide regular status reports.
	Quality & Safety: Inspect goods for damage, ensure compliance with safety standards, and maintain a clean and organized warehouse.
	Staff Supervision (if applicable): Support the Warehouse Manager in supervising warehouse staff and enforcing standard operating procedures.


Required Skills & Qualifications


	Minimum of a High School Diploma with relevant experience in stock or warehouse management.
	Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in computer usage, MS Office (Excel, Word), and inventory management software.
	Ability to multitask, work under pressure, and operate in a physically demanding environment.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.


Salary
N50,000 - N70,000 / month

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cvpool2021@oppo-aed.ng using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Tax Manager",
  "description": "Beta Glass, a proud member of the Frigoglass Group, stands as a strategic partner to international beverage bottlers. Positioned as a major supplier of glass packaging, we play a crucial role in the burgeoning markets of West and Central Africa.

Our diverse range of glass containers offers superior packaging solutions to clients across various sectors, including soft drinks, beer, spirits, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. We are committed to delivering excellence in our products. At Beta Glass, we are not just suppliers; we are partners in progress, dedicated to setting new standards in the glass packaging industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Tax Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Ensure tirnely and accurate preparation and filing of all statutory tax returns.
	Maintain up-to-date knowledge ot Nigerian tax laws and regulations and ensure company compliance.
	Spearhead and handle tax audits systematically. respond and close issues from tax audit in a timely manner.
	Liaise With tax authorities and external consultants on audits. assessments. and inquiries.
	Manage tax computations and reconciliations for monthly. quarter&quot; and annual reporting.
	Monitor changes in tax legislation and assess their impact on the business.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (AND) in Accounting, Finance
	7-10 years of relevant work experience.
	Membership of ICAN, ACCA, or CITN understanding of Nigerian tax laws and regulatory frameworks.
	Experience with tax audits, FIRS and State tax authorities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;careers@betaglass.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Tax Manager Job at Beta Glass




        
    

        
        Beta Glass, a proud member of the Frigoglass Group, stands as a strategic partner to international beverage bottlers. Positioned as a major supplier of glass packaging, we play a crucial role in the burgeoning markets of West and Central Africa.

Our diverse range of glass containers offers superior packaging solutions to clients across various sectors, including soft drinks, beer, spirits, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. We are committed to delivering excellence in our products. At Beta Glass, we are not just suppliers; we are partners in progress, dedicated to setting new standards in the glass packaging industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Tax Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location:  Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Ensure tirnely and accurate preparation and filing of all statutory tax returns.
	Maintain up-to-date knowledge ot Nigerian tax laws and regulations and ensure company compliance.
	Spearhead and handle tax audits systematically. respond and close issues from tax audit in a timely manner.
	Liaise With tax authorities and external consultants on audits. assessments. and inquiries.
	Manage tax computations and reconciliations for monthly. quarter" and annual reporting.
	Monitor changes in tax legislation and assess their impact on the business.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (AND) in Accounting, Finance
	7-10 years of relevant work experience.
	Membership of ICAN, ACCA, or CITN understanding of Nigerian tax laws and regulatory frameworks.
	Experience with tax audits, FIRS and State tax authorities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: careers@betaglass.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "title": "Accounts & Finance Manager",
  "description": "AAA Finance and Investment Company Limited is a fast growing Finance House company established in 2018 and promoted by reputable investors. AA Finance and Investment Company limited is registered and licensed by Central Bank of Nigeria as a Finance House &nbsp;to provide financial services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts &amp; Finance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (Master&rsquo;s degree is a plus) and professional certifications such as CA or ACCA are highly preferred
	5-7 years of progressive experience in accounting and financial management, preferably within the financial services sector.
	Proven track record of managing budgets, financial reporting, and audits.
	Proficient in financial management software (e.g., QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle
	Financials) and advanced knowledge of MS Excel and other financial modeling tools.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Application to:&nbsp;hiring@aaafinancegroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Accounts & Finance Manager Job at AAA Finance and Investment Company Limited




        
    

        
        AAA Finance and Investment Company Limited is a fast growing Finance House company established in 2018 and promoted by reputable investors. AA Finance and Investment Company limited is registered and licensed by Central Bank of Nigeria as a Finance House  to provide financial services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts & Finance Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (Master’s degree is a plus) and professional certifications such as CA or ACCA are highly preferred
	5-7 years of progressive experience in accounting and financial management, preferably within the financial services sector.
	Proven track record of managing budgets, financial reporting, and audits.
	Proficient in financial management software (e.g., QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle
	Financials) and advanced knowledge of MS Excel and other financial modeling tools.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Application to: hiring@aaafinancegroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "title": "Legal / Company Secretary",
  "description": "Awesome Microfinance Bank is a digital bank that offers various financial services, including savings, loans, and fund transfers. We aim to revolutionize microfinance in Nigeria by deepening financial inclusion among rural dwellers through innovation and technology.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Legal / Company Secretary

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja
Job type: Full-time (On-site)

Role Description


	The Legal / Company Secretary will be responsible for providing legal advisory services, ensuring regulatory compliance, assisting in corporate governance, and documenting company decisions.
	The role includes drafting and reviewing legal documents, maintaining statutory records, and liaising with regulatory authorities. Additionally, the secretary will coordinate board meetings, prepare minutes, and offer guidance on legal and statutory requirements.


Qualifications


	Strong legal knowledge, including corporate and regulatory compliance
	Proficiency in drafting and reviewing documents such as contracts, agreements, and other legal paperwork
	Excellent attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to maintain statutory records accurately
	Ability to coordinate meetings, prepare minutes, and ensure adherence to corporate governance standards
	Effective communication skills, both written and verbal
	Experience liaising with regulatory authorities and understanding legal processes
	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Law (LLB) and qualification to practice law
	Familiarity with financial and microfinance sectors is a strong advantage.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits package
	Opportunities for career growth and development
	Collaborative and dvnamic work environment
	Professional development and training opportunities
	Recognition and rewards for outstanding performance.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Resume and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;hr.desk@awesomemfb.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Legal / Company Secretary Job at Awesome Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Awesome Microfinance Bank is a digital bank that offers various financial services, including savings, loans, and fund transfers. We aim to revolutionize microfinance in Nigeria by deepening financial inclusion among rural dwellers through innovation and technology.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal / Company Secretary

Job Location: Abuja
Job type: Full-time (On-site)

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Legal / Company Secretary will be responsible for providing legal advisory services, ensuring regulatory compliance, assisting in corporate governance, and documenting company decisions.
	The role includes drafting and reviewing legal documents, maintaining statutory records, and liaising with regulatory authorities. Additionally, the secretary will coordinate board meetings, prepare minutes, and offer guidance on legal and statutory requirements.


Qualifications


	Strong legal knowledge, including corporate and regulatory compliance
	Proficiency in drafting and reviewing documents such as contracts, agreements, and other legal paperwork
	Excellent attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to maintain statutory records accurately
	Ability to coordinate meetings, prepare minutes, and ensure adherence to corporate governance standards
	Effective communication skills, both written and verbal
	Experience liaising with regulatory authorities and understanding legal processes
	Bachelor’s degree in Law (LLB) and qualification to practice law
	Familiarity with financial and microfinance sectors is a strong advantage.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits package
	Opportunities for career growth and development
	Collaborative and dvnamic work environment
	Professional development and training opportunities
	Recognition and rewards for outstanding performance.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Resume and Cover Letter to: hr.desk@awesomemfb.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Engineer",
  "description": "RusselSmith is a leading integrated energy services provider with a focus on asset integrity management, operations and production, technology solutions and engineering.

Our services ensure that our customers have access to reliable data about the states of their assets and that their assets continue to perform reliably and safely all through their initial design lives, and even for extended periods after their design lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria

Job Summary


	We are looking for an accountable Project Engineer to be responsible for all engineering and technical disciplines that projects involve. You will schedule, plan, forecast, resource and manage all the technical activities aiming at assuring project accuracy and quality from conception to completion.


Responsibilities


	Prepare, schedule, coordinate and monitor the assigned engineering projects
	Monitor compliance to applicable codes, practices, QA/QC policies, performance standards and specifications
	Interact daily with the clients to interpret their needs and requirements and represent them in the field
	Perform overall quality control of the work (budget, schedule, plans, personnel&rsquo;s performance) and report regularly on project status
	Assign responsibilities and mentor project team
	Cooperate and communicate effectively with project manager and other project participants to provide assistance and technical support
	Review engineering deliverables and initiate appropriate corrective actions.


Project Engineer responsibilities include:


	Preparing, scheduling, coordinating and monitoring the assigned engineering projects
	Monitoring compliance to applicable codes, practices, QA/QC policies, performance standards and specifications
	Interacting daily with the clients to interpret their needs and requirements and representing them in the field.


Qualifications and Skills


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Civil, Petroleum or related Engineering discipline)
	3&ndash;7 years of relevant experience in project engineering or related technical role
	Professional certification (e.g., COREN registration or equivalent) is an added advantage
	Strong knowledge of engineering principles, project management methodologies, and quality control processes
	Proficiency in engineering design and visualization software such as AutoCAD
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Project)
	Demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and leadership abilities
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within cross-functional teams
	Strong understanding of health, safety, and regulatory compliance standards.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@russelsmithgroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Project Engineer Job at RusselSmith




        
    

        
        RusselSmith is a leading integrated energy services provider with a focus on asset integrity management, operations and production, technology solutions and engineering.

Our services ensure that our customers have access to reliable data about the states of their assets and that their assets continue to perform reliably and safely all through their initial design lives, and even for extended periods after their design lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Engineer

Job Location: Nigeria

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an accountable Project Engineer to be responsible for all engineering and technical disciplines that projects involve. You will schedule, plan, forecast, resource and manage all the technical activities aiming at assuring project accuracy and quality from conception to completion.


Responsibilities


	Prepare, schedule, coordinate and monitor the assigned engineering projects
	Monitor compliance to applicable codes, practices, QA/QC policies, performance standards and specifications
	Interact daily with the clients to interpret their needs and requirements and represent them in the field
	Perform overall quality control of the work (budget, schedule, plans, personnel’s performance) and report regularly on project status
	Assign responsibilities and mentor project team
	Cooperate and communicate effectively with project manager and other project participants to provide assistance and technical support
	Review engineering deliverables and initiate appropriate corrective actions.


Project Engineer responsibilities include:


	Preparing, scheduling, coordinating and monitoring the assigned engineering projects
	Monitoring compliance to applicable codes, practices, QA/QC policies, performance standards and specifications
	Interacting daily with the clients to interpret their needs and requirements and representing them in the field.


Qualifications and Skills


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Civil, Petroleum or related Engineering discipline)
	3–7 years of relevant experience in project engineering or related technical role
	Professional certification (e.g., COREN registration or equivalent) is an added advantage
	Strong knowledge of engineering principles, project management methodologies, and quality control processes
	Proficiency in engineering design and visualization software such as AutoCAD
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Project)
	Demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and leadership abilities
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within cross-functional teams
	Strong understanding of health, safety, and regulatory compliance standards.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@russelsmithgroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
